steps.title: Infinitely Many Solutions Means the Equations Are Proportional
steps.content: When a system has infinitely many solutions, the two equations represent the same line. The constants show the scale factor from the first equation to the second: $$\frac{5/2}{2/7}=\frac{35}{4}$$ So every coefficient in the second equation must be $\frac{35}{4}$ times the matching coefficient in the first equation.

steps.title: Match the coefficients
steps.content: Multiplying the first equation's coefficients by $\frac{35}{4}$ gives $$g=\frac{35}{4}\cdot\frac{2}{5}=\frac{7}{2}$$ and $$k=\frac{35}{4}\cdot\frac{7}{5}=\frac{49}{4}$$ Therefore $$\frac{g}{k}=\frac{7/2}{49/4}=\frac{2}{7}$$ The exact answer is 2/7.
